message:
feat(build): hide native-only public families from browser umbrella header

Complete the browser profile surface cleanup by removing direct
inclusion of native-only module headers (fs, process, signal, server,
grpc, messaging, tls, db, blocking, io_driver) from the umbrella
header when ASX_PROFILE_BROWSER is active.

- Simplify test_browser_boundary.c includes to use the umbrella
  header directly, validating that native-only families are properly
  excluded under browser profile
- Update PROFILE_RESOURCE_CLASS_CAPABILITY_MATRIX.md to reflect that
  native-only APIs are no longer publicly declared in browser builds
- Close issue bd-pjxz

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-reg6","title":"EPIC: Rust Reference Fixture Capture Pipeline","description":"## Goal
Populate fixtures/rust_reference/ with golden outputs captured from the pinned Rust baseline
(commit 38c152405bd03e2bd9eecf178bfbbe9472fed861, toolchain nightly-2026-02-19).

## Why This Matters
The conformance gate currently runs against the C implementation's own fixtures, which validates
internal consistency but NOT Rust-vs-C behavioral parity. Without Rust-captured reference fixtures,
we cannot prove the C port produces identical semantic outputs for identical inputs. This is the
single highest-value remaining gap — it converts "self-consistent" into "provably equivalent."

## What "Done" Looks Like
- fixtures/rust_reference/ contains captured golden outputs for all 14 E2E fixture families (85+ scenarios)
- Each fixture has full provenance metadata (rust_baseline_commit, toolchain_commit_hash, cargo_lock_sha256)
- make conformance --fixtures-root fixtures/rust_reference passes with 0 semantic deltas
- Fixture schema validation passes against schemas/canonical_fixture.schema.json

## Architectural Context
The conformance infrastructure (tools/ci/run_conformance.sh) already supports --fixtures-root pointing
at Rust-captured data. The fixture schema, provenance map (docs/SOURCE_TO_FIXTURE_PROVENANCE_MAP.md,
28 provenance rows), and semantic delta budget policy (docs/SEMANTIC_DELTA_BUDGET.md, budget=0) are
all in place. This epic is purely about running the Rust side and capturing output.

## Risk
- The pinned Rust baseline may not build cleanly on current nightly (last verified 2026-02-26)
- Cargo.lock SHA must match exactly: 77f4bfaedfe5b8572dfca3181119f9432825ea3d868ee8f5d2793a6c5cdeffdc
- Any Rust-vs-C semantic delta discovered here would be a real bug (in either direction)","notes":"## IMPORTANT: FIXTURE DIRECTORY IS NOT EMPTY (2026-03-12 audit)

The fixtures/rust_reference/ directory IS populated with 7 families:
- smoke/ (10 fixtures), core_budget/, core_cancel/, core_channel/,
  core_lifecycle/, core_timer/, deployment_hardening/

HOWEVER, these are PLACEHOLDER/SCAFFOLD fixtures generated by the C implementation:
- semantic_digest values are "sha256:aaaaaaa..." (all-a placeholder)
- expected_events are empty arrays
- Provenance fields reference the correct Rust baseline but fixtures were NOT
  generated by actually running the Rust binary

The FEATURE_PARITY_VERIFICATION report (Gap #1) is correct: no actual Rust-generated
fixtures exist. The directory contains structural scaffolds.

## What This Epic Does
Replace placeholder fixtures with REAL Rust-generated golden outputs by:
1. Setting up Rust build environment
2. Building the Rust binary in fixture capture mode
3. Running it to generate real fixtures (replacing placeholders)
4. Validating the real fixtures against the schema
5. Running C conformance against real Rust fixtures (first true parity test)
6. E2E test verifying the entire pipeline

## Dependency Structure
Linear: .1 → .2 → .3 → .4 → .5, then .6 (e2e test) depends on .5

## Cross-Epic Dependencies
- bd-rkql depends on bd-reg6 (fuzzing needs Rust binary)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","estimated_minutes":600,"created_at":"2026-03-12T17:05:50.816361571Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T01:40:12.333091287Z","closed_at":"2026-03-13T01:40:12.331096181Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","phase3"]}

@@ -268,7 +268,7 @@ The table below maps the crate-root feature/platform restrictions from
 | Upstream contract | Current ANSI C equivalent | State | Evidence / gap |
 |---|---|---|---|
 | `wasm32` must choose exactly one canonical browser profile | `ASX_PROFILE_BROWSER` exists in `include/asx/asx_config.h`, `src/runtime/profile_compat.c`, the explicit `build-browser` / `PROFILE=BROWSER` lane, and the focused `test-browser-focused` CI/test lane in `Makefile` | `partial` | The browser build/test surface is now explicit and CI-covered, and `include/asx/asx_config.h` now hard-fails multiple `ASX_PROFILE_*` selections while declaring `ASX_BROWSER_PROFILE_MODE_COUNT == 1`, but there is still no compile-time browser subprofile split because the C port only exposes one browser mode today |
-| `native-runtime` forbidden on browser builds | Browser boundary gates native surfaces and compile-time capability macros mark them unavailable | `partial` | `include/asx/runtime/browser_boundary.h` fail-closes filesystem/process/signal/io/blocking plus the shipped `server`/`grpc`/`messaging` families, and `include/asx/asx_config.h` now exposes `ASX_HAS_NATIVE_RUNTIME_SURFACES == 0` under browser builds, but native-only APIs are still publicly declared rather than removed from the browser umbrella surface |
+| `native-runtime` forbidden on browser builds | Browser boundary gates native surfaces, compile-time capability macros mark them unavailable, and the browser umbrella stops directly advertising native-only families | `partial` | `include/asx/runtime/browser_boundary.h` fail-closes filesystem/process/signal/io/blocking plus the shipped `server`/`grpc`/`messaging` families, `include/asx/asx_config.h` exposes `ASX_HAS_NATIVE_RUNTIME_SURFACES == 0` under browser builds, and `include/asx/asx.h` now omits direct inclusion of `fs`/`process`/`signal`, `server`, `grpc`, `messaging`, `tls`, `db`, `blocking`, and `io_driver` when `ASX_PROFILE_BROWSER` is active; remaining debt is transitive native leakage through browser-safe headers such as `http.h` plus the lack of finer-grained subprofile removal |
 | `browser-io` forbidden with upstream minimal browser profile | Native I/O blocked under browser boundary and compile-time capability macros | `partial` | `ASX_SURFACE_IO_DRIVER` is denied in browser mode and `include/asx/asx_config.h` now exposes `ASX_HAS_NATIVE_IO_DRIVER == 0`, but there is still no separate browser-IO feature flag or profile-minimal compile check |
 | `browser-trace` forbidden with upstream minimal browser profile | No separate browser trace feature | `gap` | The C tree has browser diagnostics and trace support, but no compile-time browser trace subprofile split |
 | `cli` unsupported on browser builds | No `cli` module in C | `gap` | The C port currently lacks a public CLI family entirely, so there is no feature-gated fail-closed browser rule to enforce |
